name: SPJ Foundation Grants
eligibility: Journalists, media professionals (incl. freelancers) and organizations; individuals may apply with a project proposal.
description: The 2026 cycle funds projects that defend press freedom, build newsroom resilience, advance media literacy and public trust. Recent individual awards ranged from ~$1,871 to $20,000. Open now.
viabilityNote: Individuals eligible but competitive (~7% of proposals funded). Action: submit a tight project proposal before the Aug 13 2026 deadline.
relevant2026Note: 2026 grant cycle is open now with a firm Aug 13, 2026 deadline.

name: NEH Public Scholars Program
deadline: Annual; next cycle expected ~April 2027 (2026 deadline Apr 22 has closed)
eligibility: Individual writers with or without academic affiliation; no advanced degree required, but must have previously published at least one sole-authored nonfiction book.
description: Direct-to-individual grants for researching and writing a well-researched nonfiction book in the humanities for a general audience. NEH explicitly encourages independent writers, researchers and journalists. Paid to the applicant, not an institution.
viabilityNote: Individual-eligible and cash-rich, but requires a prior published book and a competitive proposal. Action: prepare a sample chapter/outline for the ~April 2027 cycle.
relevant2026Note: 2026 round closed Apr 22; the program runs annually so the next window is expected around April 2027.

name: Linda Hall Library Fellowships
amount: $3,000/month (doctoral) to $4,200/month (postdoc), up to 4 months; residential or virtual
deadline: Annual; next cycle expected ~January 2027 (2026–27 deadline Jan 19 has closed)
eligibility: Graduate students, postdoctoral researchers and independent scholars in the history of science, technology, engineering and related humanities.
description: Residential (Kansas City) or virtual fellowships supporting research using the Library's science and engineering collections. Independent scholars are explicitly eligible — no institutional sponsor required.
viabilityNote: Open to independent scholars; relatively simple application but topic must fit the collections. Action: align a history-of-science/technology project to the collection and apply for the ~Jan 2027 deadline.
relevant2026Note: 2026–27 round closed Jan 19, 2026; the program runs annually with the next call expected late 2026 / Jan 2027.

name: IJNR Field Reporting Grants
amount: Up to $500 (Steve Dolberg Memorial grant up to $2,500)
deadline: Annual; next cycle expected ~February 2027 (2026 deadline Feb 27 has closed)
eligibility: Working journalists including freelancers; projects must cover natural resources/environment/energy/agriculture/EJ/public health and be based in or related to North America.
description: Small, fast grants to defray reporting costs (travel, lodging, research; up to 20% for compensation) for environmental/natural-resource stories. Freelancers explicitly eligible — very low application burden.
viabilityNote: Open to individual freelancers with a simple proposal. Action: pitch an environmental reporting project for the next (~Feb 2027) round; small but easy money.
relevant2026Note: 2026 round closed Feb 27; runs annually so the next call is expected ~Feb 2027.

name: GameDev.tv Indie Game Dev Fund
deadline: Next round opens Q3 or Q4 2026
eligibility: Indie developers or small teams with a playable prototype or clear work-in-progress; individuals eligible.
description: Recurring micro-funding boosts for indie game developers; recipients share progress with the community. First 2026 round awarded five $500 sponsorships; next round announced for Q3/Q4 2026.
viabilityNote: Solo devs eligible, simple form. Action: have a playable prototype ready and apply when the Q3/Q4 2026 round opens; small but low-effort cash.
relevant2026Note: Applications currently closed; next round explicitly slated for Q3/Q4 2026.

name: OpenStreetMap US Microgrants
amount: Microgrants funding events, travel and educational/mapping initiatives
eligibility: OpenStreetMap US members; individuals may apply.
description: Always-open, rolling-review microgrants that fund community events, travel and educational initiatives strengthening OpenStreetMap across the US. Low-friction application reviewed on a rolling basis.
viabilityNote: Open to individual members on a rolling basis with a light application. Action: become an OSM US member and submit a focused community/mapping proposal anytime.
relevant2026Note: Rolling program accepting applications continuously through 2026.

name: Lycée Français de Chicago Artist-in-Residence
deadline: Annual; next cycle expected ~April 2027 (2026–27 deadline Apr 15 has closed)
eligibility: Working professional artist (any discipline, incl. digital/film/animation/design), speaks French and/or English, prior school experience preferred, background check required.
description: A funded school residency giving one artist a $15,000 unrestricted stipend to develop work while engaging students. All disciplines welcome including digital and media arts.
viabilityNote: Individual artists eligible but niche (French/English, school engagement, in-person Chicago). Action: prepare an inclusive school-based project for the ~April 2027 call.
relevant2026Note: 2026–27 round closed Apr 15, 2026; runs annually with the next call expected ~spring 2027.

name: Anolic Family Awards
organization: Anolic Family Award (via Jewish Art Salon)
deadline: Annual; next cycle expected ~May–June 2027 (2026 deadlines closed early June)
eligibility: Early-career Jewish visual artists (ages 25–35) and Jewish students; free to apply.
description: Three small, unrestricted annual awards for early-career Jewish artists: the Naomi Anolic visual arts award ($1,000), the Isaac Anolic book arts award ($1,500), and the Bertha Anolic Israel study award ($2,000). No application fee, light application.
viabilityNote: Individual-eligible and low-effort, but restricted to early-career Jewish artists. Action: prepare a project sample for the next (~mid-2027) cycle.
relevant2026Note: 2026 deadlines closed in early June; awards recur annually with the next window expected ~May/June 2027.
